patch.value:
Promoted from the pm-mpbb DX ledger (candidate lever a) on 2026-07-11, when the pm-a22j baseline gate opened (full AC met: checked-in script scripts/measure-agent-token-surface.mjs + MCP tools/list = 32,979 B ~= 8.2k tok / 28 tools).

The a22j baseline measures the cost of the SURFACE (help/contracts/tools-list payload sizes). Nothing measures what an agent actually pays per completed task: tokens read + retries burned across a realistic command sequence. Without that, DX regressions (verbose new output, envelope bloat, extra confirmation rounds) stay invisible until a human notices.

Approach (per the ledger fence: extend, never a new harness):
- Record golden agent transcripts (ordered pm command sequences with expected envelopes) for a handful of canonical tasks: triage-and-claim, create-with-deps, close-with-resolution, context bootstrap.
- Replay them against the current build and report total bytes/approx-tokens per completed task.
- Wire the result into the existing token-cost CI gate lineage (pm-cu1i, shipped PR#487) with a documented regression threshold, exactly like the changelog and contracts drift gates.

Boundary: pm-atfm (related) grades context/next assembly QUALITY with rank-aware metrics; this item measures token COST per task. Keep the corpora separate; the replay runner may share golden-workspace fixtures.
Golden agent-transcript replay: measure tokens-per-completed-task as a CI-visible DX regression gate

Golden transcript corpus (canonical agent task sequences) versioned in-repo; replay runner reports bytes+approx-tokens per completed task against the current build; wired into the existing token-cost CI gate lineage with a documented regression threshold; boundary with pm-atfm quality harness documented; workflow for adding transcripts documented
